action.reasoning: Observation: best full-test exploration_score=0.6057 (success_rate=0.8733, travel=337.7m on 150 maps; rooms at 0.8 success). Recent local runs use proxy (9 maps, optimistic scores ~0.60) due to predict.py truncation at fallback logic (NameError risk) + incomplete run_evaluation. Analysis: the global Dijkstra-based (utility**beta)/(dist**alpha+1) target + next-hop on node graph is a strong standard frontier planner (0 params, fully respects ExplorationEnv, node utilities, <=50M, offline contract). Root cause of instability=long code leading to repeated workspace truncation. New alpha=2.5/beta=1.5 emphasizes high-utility clusters slightly more for potential room improvements/lower avg dist. Decision: overwrite with FULL compact non-truncated predict.py (cleaned select_waypoint with np.linalg.norm, all fallbacks, one dijkstra+prev, exact contract JSON, correct (1/N)sum S*(300/d) formula, robust config parse or glob fallback to 3 maps/type proxy, per-ep try/except). Update train.py with new params+version. Run train+proxy validation, then submit_eval on checkpoint to obtain official full 150-map test score.
action.command: python train.py && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline --output output.json
